creator: curt@tkg.com
text:
i had a ps/2 mouse.  even though dmesg said it was not detected, a
/dev/mse0 device was created.  'cat /dev/mse0' crashes the machine.

such user command should not be able to crash the system if we want
freebsd to achieve a reputation of robustness.  (this is the kind of
thing we would fix in aix for sure)

Fix: 

maybe spec_open() needs to be a little more careful?  not sure.


The dump and kernel available for analysis from 
ftp://ftp.tkg.com/private/curt/crash.tgz  12 megs gzipped.

How-To-Repeat: boot a dell dimension on a kernel where psm0 is not defined but should 
be.  type 'cat /dev/mse0'
